reviews.review_text: Great game, however you pretty much experiance everything in 12+- hours, gets repetetive when trying to go for a 100% (wish me luck).

IT IS NOT A GAME. IT IS AN EXPERIENCE. 

+ + + An experience about the cruelty of war. About the suffering and torment of ordinary civilians. Unfortunately, it is still a very relevant topic today.... 
Not a game you will find fun to play. But that is not its purpose. The devs made this game based on the personal stories of survivors of the blody Siege of Sarajevo. Strongly recommend. The first playthrough will hit.

+ + + Atmosphere is true art. The game has been remastered, so you can barely tell it is already 12 years old.

- - - The game will not tell you much about how to play. Also the combat system is not easy to learn. Charaters will die. Once you did 2-3 playthrough and played the DLC stories (very good ones) the game does not much to offer as you already know how everything works. 
It is not for everyone I know. Still a solid experience for only 3€ on sale. 

(I played it a lot 10 years ago and just got it on Steam as well. Definitely on my top 10 game list.)

Top tier game.

Some of the events and endings REALLY punches you in the feels.
Watching a child starve/freeze to death because your only adults have died/left/too depressed, is not the most enjoyable thing to witness. Learn from your mistakes and press on.

Difficulty is as hard as you want it to be - you can take the safe route or be a murder-hobo, both choices have consequences.

Pros: Excellent re-playability, convincing and compelling atmosphere. Graphics that perfectly match the setting and environment. Great and memorable soundtrack. Amazing story telling through small notes and character monologues. Realistic combat / exploration system.
Cons: Inability to increase / reduce in-game speed.

What can I say.... this was the first time i ever played a game like this. (more into starcraft, total war , warhammer, fps etc games) and was absolutely instantly hooked. Its a playstyle I never encountered before but easily able too figure out. The style of choosing too be good or evil, or too steal/not steal, kill/leave alive. all the while has base building in it also. I highly suggest watching gameplay online because reading another persons review on it can only go so far. but IMO buy it. And again IMO only if its on sale....but that has nothing too do with the game. That is my preference towards all games. Solid 8/10 I'd like too see more areas but I doubt this game will go any further since its been released in 2014, cool mods also i suggest checking those out because it fixes stuff like how much of the same item you can carry which comes in very handy when you go on raids, and also more weapons and things too create gives it more diversity and replay ability.                                            -FIN

Such an excellent game.  Don't be afraid to sink some major time into this game.  There's quite a bit to discover and it's very satisfying.  Keep priority on keeping your guys fed, safe and sane.  Don't go gung ho ever.  Grab essentials.  Get your ass back.  Make your place safe first and foremost with food a close second.  Only upgrade as the situation calls for.  Each scenario will alter the needs a bit.  Just be conservative in your choices for the most part.  Don't upgrade something if you don't even need it to the detriment of security or well being.  Very addictive and I place it up there is some the best games I've played in 40 years..

Actually such an awesome game. Chill, but has moments of tense. Modding community is awesome and it is a complete game. I do wish some new developer stories came out or scenarios even if they were DLC based. I can imagine this type of game would work well with a little zombie apocolypse DLC. Also the art style is cool!

i nice game to reminisce about the horrors of war, and what can war do humans

Great game! Very nice atmosphere and it's easy to get into it... just to lose everything when you get way too comfortable and think you're invincible (you're not). 

The DLC is... ok and all. The game is way more fun when you can do whatever you want without having to follow a story. But it's fun, nonetheless.

One of the most emotionally intense survival games I’ve ever played. The dark atmosphere, art style, and soundtrack create constant tension, while the survival mechanics never feel repetitive. Every scavenging run feels risky and rewarding, and the moral choices are genuinely difficult. There’s usually no “right” answer. The pacing between managing your shelter during the day and dangerous night missions is excellent. A brutal, memorable, and incredibly well-designed game.
It's not a true survival game since it's broken up into scenarios/campaigns with lots of repeating areas just from different POV's, it is however atmospheric and charming, 6/10
Great game with amazing replay value. I always end up playing as an axe murderer of bandits.

Its a great survival sim game.

But there's too much RNG BS in it. This really drags the whole thing down.

I think this is a powerful, appealing piece of art.
However, I don't enjoy playing it.

Gameplay wise, it's really simple, the strategy of it really is not very complex and can be easily solved, and completely shortcut through just one guide or video of an experienced player. There is not much depth there. It really is not all that fun for me.

Now here's the thing. It's incredibly meaningful, well made, and stylistically beautiful. Why do I not recommend this right now? The gameplay is more than half the game and it is presented as a strategy game, when the real strength here is the artistic nature, ethics, morality, and story telling.

The game's gameplay is just not compelling enough for me to keep playing.

Can't say it's bad, but I don't think it's for me - the combination of slow pace and high stakes is not something I like. Some things are unrealistic enough to pull me out of the game: you cannot scavenge with more than one person, making bandages requires pure alcohol and herbs, you cannot disassemble large furniture to make fuel, you cannot boil water to purify it. Also the controls - mouse drags move the screen but clicks direct the characters, so it's way to easy to accidentally send a person somewhere while scrolling the map. Some actions take away your characters for the whole day, even when the description sounds easy. And so on.

Don't buy this game, It's not worth paying even a cent for it, they buff the NPC up so you can't even defend from them and most of them carry guns when you have fist or if your lucky a crowbar. just a horrible game. I figured it would be a fun game nope not at all you never have enough food and it takes SO much to just make one simple meal, and It takes forever to walk around, like there just slowing doing stuff while the NPC  move faster and always have guns that almost kill you in one shot. people who made this game must be happy to scam people out of there hard earned money,  SO many games on steam follow this same path, Just shows you how far Steam has declined in recent years. And Again DON'T BUY THIS GAME IT'S A SCAM, AND THE MODS ARE NOT VERY GOOD ETHER. HORRIBLE GAME.........WOULD OF NEVER GOT IT IF I KNEW HOW BAD IT REALLY WAS.

frustrating, tiring and boring. i really don't care about the message they are trying to give anymore the game is so dragging that it ruined it for me. played it years ago and gave up on it shortly after. felt like i should give it another chance and my god young me was right this game is a waste of time. wouldn't have played it even if it was free wish i could remove it from my account

Strip away the virtue signalling about war, and what is left?
A simple and rather dull survival game!
Bought this to use with a controller, especially through steam link on phone.  It's an absolute nightmare to control.  No

[h1]Yes, because:[/h1]
[list]
[*]Unique experience
[*]Moral dilemmas that genuinely make you uncomfortable
[*]Emotional storytelling that hits hard and stays with you
[*]Beautiful art style and atmosphere, characters with real depth
[/list]
[h1]But:[/h1]
[list]
[*]Warning – mentally heavy
[*]Gets repetitive over longer runs
[*]…but honestly, nothing major
[/list]

Oh, wow, with 132 hours of play, I haven't given this the review it deserves, or rather, the empathy victims of war deserve regardless of your proximity to it and them.

"This Was of Mine" ensnared me. The title itself gives credence to each individual and their physical, internal, and external struggles to survive based on their own histories, needs, and current availability of sustainable goods and comforts. War happens *to* you. It happens *around* you. It's an accident of placement as much as it is tragically personal. This game captures these elements well.

Being that is is ten years old, I wonder if the devs have new ideas for delivering such a graphically personal view of war. It's not that this game feels weathered, but rather, story-telling has evolved so much, I'm interested in the balance of delivery between personal story and the next topic: war itself.

I think this game struck me when I was younger because it was genuinely foreign - it was my own curiosity for suffering that lead me to sink so many hours into this game.

That is a profoundly privileged entry fee: to "enjoy" war from a distance. It's foul. It's cruel. It's so frustratingly human. And, I think, an incredible lesson for anyone who plays this game. The events, desperation, and personal considerations are real to someone. Right now. In the next hour. Tomorrow. One bomb dropped on your city due to circumstances completely out of your control makes the difference between starvation, deprivation, and suffering so profound, you wish you were dead. This is not outside of the scope of the game.

This is an important game.

It was the beginning of an exorcism from my white girl middle class existence. Alright, it was all I knew. Yep, I had a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ up childhood and that matters very much - I didn't have it easy. But this isn't about the Pain Olympics and who has it worse, Boomer. The point is that my view of the world was ease, was "slavery bad," was "eat your food - there are starving kids in Africa." This game made me realize that the oversimplification of suffering is part of the problem.

Suffering is universal. But war? War is a CHOICE.

There's a lot more to say on all of these topics. A lot more.

The unfair paraphrase is this: "This War of Mine" tore me out of ignorance via deeply personal metaphor and literalism. If it doesn't shake you, at minimum, something is deeply, introspectively wrong with you. It's everyone's responsibility to participate in rebuking war's many forms not for the sake of personal insurance, but because there is too much potential trapped behind contrived "enemy" lines. Although there is no difference between you and someone who is scavenging for food as you "enjoy" this game, the only factor separating you and devastation are the conditions of your birth and, if you have such a privilege, your chosen placement.

War isn't hell - it's a preventable, man-made pathogen.
